title: phlox drummondii var. tharpii
intro: Phlox drummondii var. tharpii, also known as Drummond's phlox or blue phlox, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and its medicinal properties include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phlox drummondii var. tharpii include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
